question_text: Which statement correctly compares the speeds of two runners? Runner A covers 60 metres in 10 seconds, and Runner B covers 80 metres in 10 seconds.

options.label: A
options.text: Runner A is faster because they run for the same time

options.label: B
options.text: Runner B is faster because they cover more distance in the same time

options.label: C
options.text: They run at the same speed

options.label: D
options.text: Runner A is faster because 60 is less than 80

explanation: Runner B covers more distance (80 m) than Runner A (60 m) in the same time (10 seconds), so Runner B is faster. When two objects travel for the same time, the one covering more distance is moving faster.
